entities.name: CONSERVATION AGRICULTURE
entities.description: A climate-smart farming approach based on minimal soil disturbance, organic soil cover, and crop rotations

entities.name: SOUTHERN AFRICA
entities.description: The geographical region where the research on smallholder farms was conducted

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: The long-term shift in global or regional climate patterns posing threats to agriculture

entities.name: CLIMATE-SMART AGRICULTURE
entities.description: An integrated approach to managing landscapes to address the three goals of productivity, adaptation, and mitigation

entities.name: ADAPTATION
entities.description: The process of adjusting agricultural systems to be more resilient to climate effects

entities.name: MITIGATION
entities.description: The process of reducing the emission of greenhouse gases or enhancing their sinks

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of agricultural output and income generated from farming systems

entities.name: CARBON
entities.description: An element sequestered in soil to mitigate climate change effects

entities.name: GREENHOUSE GAS EMISSIONS
entities.description: Gases that trap heat in the atmosphere, which need to be reduced for climate mitigation

entities.name: INFILTRATION RATES
entities.description: The rate at which water enters the soil, maintained by conservation agriculture

entities.name: SOIL MOISTURE
entities.description: The water content of the soil, conserved by conservation agriculture

entities.name: SEASONAL DRY-SPELLS
entities.description: Periods of abnormally dry weather during the growing season

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The amount of crop produced per unit area

entities.name: SOIL ORGANIC C
entities.description: The organic carbon stored in the soil, used as an indicator of mitigation

entities.name: SURFACE RESIDUE RETENTION
entities.description: The practice of keeping crop residues on the soil surface

entities.name: INTERCROPPING
entities.description: The practice of growing two or more crops simultaneously on the same field

entities.name: AGROFORESTRY
entities.description: The integration of trees and shrubs into crop and livestock systems

entities.name: TREE-BASED SYSTEMS
entities.description: Agricultural systems that incorporate trees to improve environmental benefits
